meta.certificationName: Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer
meta.title: GCP-MLE-2: Vertex AI Platform, Pipelines and Monitoring

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **manage reusable features for both training and low-latency serving**?

options.text: Use batch prediction jobs as the feature repository
Use Cloud Logging only
Use Vertex AI Pipelines only
Use Vertex AI Feature Store

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Feature Store is the managed capability for serving and reusing features across training and online scenarios. **A** is incorrect because prediction jobs consume features rather than acting as a feature catalog **B** is incorrect because logs are not designed to serve online or offline features **C** is incorrect because pipelines orchestrate workflow steps but are not the feature repository itself

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **manage reusable features for both training and low-latency serving**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Use Vertex AI Feature Store
Use Vertex AI Pipelines only
Use Cloud Logging only
Use batch prediction jobs as the feature repository

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Feature Store is the managed capability for serving and reusing features across training and online scenarios. **B** is incorrect because pipelines orchestrate workflow steps but are not the feature repository itself **C** is incorrect because logs are not designed to serve online or offline features **D** is incorrect because prediction jobs consume features rather than acting as a feature catalog

questions.stem: A project lead wants to use the managed platform capability that best supports **manage reusable features for both training and low-latency serving**. What should the team choose?

options.text: Use Vertex AI Feature Store
Use Cloud Logging only

Use batch prediction jobs as the feature repository

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Feature Store is the managed capability for serving and reusing features across training and online scenarios. **B** is incorrect because logs are not designed to serve online or offline features **C** is incorrect because pipelines orchestrate workflow steps but are not the feature repository itself **D** is incorrect because prediction jobs consume features rather than acting as a feature catalog

questions.stem: A deployment is underperforming because the wrong approach was chosen. If the real requirement is **manage reusable features for both training and low-latency serving**, which correction is BEST?

options.text: Use batch prediction jobs as the feature repository
Use Vertex AI Feature Store
Use Vertex AI Pipelines only
Use Cloud Logging only

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Feature Store is the managed capability for serving and reusing features across training and online scenarios. **A** is incorrect because prediction jobs consume features rather than acting as a feature catalog **C** is incorrect because pipelines orchestrate workflow steps but are not the feature repository itself **D** is incorrect because logs are not designed to serve online or offline features

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **orchestrate repeatable ML steps with lineage and reusable components**?

options.text: Use a single online endpoint for every step
Use Vertex AI Pipelines
Use model monitoring alerts as the orchestrator
Use only ad hoc notebooks

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Pipelines provide orchestration, lineage, and reusable components for repeatable ML workflows. **A** is incorrect because online endpoints serve inference rather than orchestrate workflows **C** is incorrect because monitoring detects issues but is not the pipeline engine **D** is incorrect because manual notebook execution is harder to reproduce and operate

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **return predictions synchronously for an interactive application**?

options.text: Run a nightly batch prediction job
Deploy an online prediction endpoint
Use hyperparameter tuning instead of serving
Export predictions to Cloud Storage once per week

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because online endpoints are designed for low-latency synchronous inference requests. **A** is incorrect because batch jobs are for offline scoring, not request-time responses **C** is incorrect because tuning optimizes model search rather than exposes inference **D** is incorrect because weekly exports are stale for interactive use

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **return predictions synchronously for an interactive application**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Export predictions to Cloud Storage once per week
Use hyperparameter tuning instead of serving
Run a nightly batch prediction job
Deploy an online prediction endpoint

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because online endpoints are designed for low-latency synchronous inference requests. **A** is incorrect because weekly exports are stale for interactive use **B** is incorrect because tuning optimizes model search rather than exposes inference **C** is incorrect because batch jobs are for offline scoring, not request-time responses

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **score a very large dataset offline without a human waiting for each result**?

options.text: Deploy a single-user notebook
Run a batch prediction job
Use an online endpoint for every row interactively
Use model monitoring only

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because batch prediction is intended for large asynchronous scoring workloads against datasets. **A** is incorrect because notebooks are not the managed large-scale scoring path **C** is incorrect because online inference is inefficient for bulk offline scoring **D** is incorrect because monitoring observes models but does not perform scoring

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **score a very large dataset offline without a human waiting for each result**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Use an online endpoint for every row interactively
Run a batch prediction job
Deploy a single-user notebook
Use model monitoring only

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because batch prediction is intended for large asynchronous scoring workloads against datasets. **A** is incorrect because online inference is inefficient for bulk offline scoring **C** is incorrect because notebooks are not the managed large-scale scoring path **D** is incorrect because monitoring observes models but does not perform scoring

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **detect feature drift or prediction skew after a model is deployed**?

options.text: Use Vertex AI Model Monitoring
Rely on training accuracy from last month
Use only BigQuery scheduled queries with no model telemetry
Increase endpoint machine size

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Model Monitoring is built to observe deployed models for skew, drift, and related serving-time issues. **B** is incorrect because historic training metrics do not show live drift **C** is incorrect because generic queries can help analysis but do not replace the managed monitoring feature **D** is incorrect because bigger machines affect capacity, not drift detection

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **detect feature drift or prediction skew after a model is deployed**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Increase endpoint machine size
Use Vertex AI Model Monitoring
Rely on training accuracy from last month
Use only BigQuery scheduled queries with no model telemetry

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Model Monitoring is built to observe deployed models for skew, drift, and related serving-time issues. **A** is incorrect because bigger machines affect capacity, not drift detection **C** is incorrect because historic training metrics do not show live drift **D** is incorrect because generic queries can help analysis but do not replace the managed monitoring feature

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **search model hyperparameters automatically to improve validation performance**?

options.text: Skip validation and pick random values
Use Vertex AI hyperparameter tuning
Change the prediction endpoint region
Create more dashboards

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because hyperparameter tuning automates exploration of parameter combinations against an objective metric. **A** is incorrect because unguarded random choice is not a reliable optimization method **C** is incorrect because region changes latency, not model search quality **D** is incorrect because dashboards do not optimize hyperparameters

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **search model hyperparameters automatically to improve validation performance**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Change the prediction endpoint region

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **give practitioners a managed notebook environment for exploration and prototyping**?

options.text: Use a batch prediction job
Use Model Monitoring
Use only a production endpoint
Use Vertex AI Workbench

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because Workbench provides managed notebooks integrated with Vertex AI workflows. **A** is incorrect because batch prediction is not an interactive development workspace **B** is incorrect because monitoring is for deployed models, not exploration **C** is incorrect because serving infrastructure is not a notebook environment

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **give practitioners a managed notebook environment for exploration and prototyping**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Use only a production endpoint

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **choose a single managed platform for building, training, serving, and governing ML workloads on Google Cloud**?

options.text: Use Vertex AI as the unified ML platform
Use Looker dashboards as the primary ML platform
Build separate custom services for every lifecycle stage by default
Use Cloud Storage as the only ML platform component

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Vertex AI is positioned as Google Cloud's managed platform spanning major ML lifecycle tasks. **B** is incorrect because BI tooling is not the central ML lifecycle platform **C** is incorrect because fragmentation increases operational burden when a managed platform is available **D** is incorrect because object storage is only one supporting building block

questions.stem: Which TWO statements are correct about **review a deployed model for production issues before they become user-visible**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use endpoint CPU metrics as the only model-quality signal
Track drift or skew signals from live traffic
Assume the model is stable forever after successful training
Define alerting or review workflows around monitoring output

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **D** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **A** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption

questions.stem: A team is reviewing design choices for **review a deployed model for production issues before they become user-visible**. Which TWO choices are valid? (Select TWO)

options.text: Define alerting or review workflows around monitoring output
Use endpoint CPU metrics as the only model-quality signal
Assume the model is stable forever after successful training
Track drift or skew signals from live traffic

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because alerts and review loops turn monitoring observations into operational action **D** is correct because live-serving signals help reveal when production data no longer matches training expectations **B** is incorrect because resource metrics matter operationally but do not alone measure model behavior **C** is incorrect because production data changes over time and can invalidate that assumption

questions.stem: Which TWO statements are correct about **move from a one-off notebook experiment to a production-ready repeatable training pipeline**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Package the steps as pipeline components with defined inputs and outputs
Rerun notebook cells manually whenever production retraining is needed
Store the only copy of the workflow in a developer's local session
Record artifacts and execution history through the managed orchestration system

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because componentized steps are easier to automate and reuse **D** is correct because lineage and execution records are core parts of operational ML workflows **B** is incorrect because manual execution is brittle and hard to audit **C** is incorrect because local-only workflow definitions are not production-ready
